# Brutalist Framework (retro-brutalist-framework)

**Upstream:** https://github.com/pinecreativelabs/Brutalist-Framework
**Demo site:** https://brutalistframework.com/ — ALSO ships in-repo at ./static/*.html (40+ component pages)
**License:** "BITCHY License" — effectively MIT for code (see LICENSE.txt)

## What it looks like
Raw web brutalism (older school — closer to the original brutalism wave than neobrutalism.dev):
system fonts, default browser underlines, high-density content walls, unstyled-looking but controlled,
monospace mixed with serif, stark black/white + one accent red. Looks like a 1995 university website
doing it on purpose. Heavier text, lighter on color.

## What it offers
- Two delivery modes:
  - ./static — HTML-only, 40+ self-contained .html pages (3d-cubes, bad-assets, bfx, bicons, boilerplate,
    buff, buix, butch, filetypes, final-countdown, flavors, final-countdown, gnarly, grids, loopy, mocks,
    noisy, old-school, pg-parry, proto, punk, quirk, retro, savage, sloppy, twist, typography, urb,
    vexed, winter, xp, yolo, zany, zest + app/core libraries)
  - ./dynamic — server-rendered version (requires backend)
- Dense component palette: tables, forms, lists, type specimens, decorative noise/dither textures
- Philosophy is "no hiding" — all borders visible, all cursor states default

## Clawmachine fit
HIGH — this is ACTUAL brutalism (as opposed to neobrutalism.dev's style-trend-brutalism).
Pairs with system.css (classic mac) for a genuine raw aesthetic rather than trend-pastiche.
./static pages are reference gallery material — 40 HTML variants of what brutalist web can be.
Good source of typography and density studies.
